# --- Case study fields (all optional) ---

role: Solo Developer
timeframe: Oct 2025 – Jan 2026

stack:
  - TypeScript
  - React
  - Node.js
  - PostgreSQL

highlights:
  - Built a real-time dashboard with WebSocket updates
  - Reduced API response times by 60% through query optimization
  - Implemented role-based access control for 3 user tiers
  - Achieved 98% test coverage across critical paths

results:
  - 500+ daily active users within first month
  - 99.9% uptime over 6 months
  - 4.8/5 average user satisfaction rating

problem: >
  Teams needed a centralized way to track project progress across departments,
  but existing tools were either too complex or lacked real-time collaboration
  features.

constraints:
  - Must work offline with sync-on-reconnect
  - Budget limited to free-tier infrastructure
  - Accessibility compliance required (WCAG 2.1 AA)

approach:
  - Started with user interviews to identify core pain points
  - Built an MVP focused on real-time status boards
  - Iterated based on weekly feedback from pilot users
  - Gradually added offline support using service workers

architecture:
  - React SPA with Context API for state management
  - Express.js REST API with WebSocket layer
  - PostgreSQL with row-level security policies
  - Redis for session caching and pub/sub

challenges:
  - Handling concurrent edits required conflict resolution strategy
  - Offline sync introduced eventual consistency edge cases
  - Balancing feature requests with maintaining simplicity

lessons:
  - Start with the simplest sync model and add complexity only when needed
  - User interviews surface requirements that analytics miss
  - Accessibility constraints often improve the design for everyone

nextSteps:
  - Add mobile app with React Native
  - Integrate with Slack and Teams for notifications
  - Build analytics dashboard for project health metrics
---
